symptom: TDO always returns 0x00000000, IDCODE reads as 0x00000000
root_cause: CPLD version 0xFFFE — TDO path through CPLD is dead, cable cannot read back
lesson: If IDCODE is all-zeros, check CPLD version first — it's a hardware path issue, not a software bug

action: Load FX2 firmware via fxload to switch PID 0x0013 -> 0x0008
symptom: Cable initially shows as PID 0x0013 (unconfigured FX2)
root_cause: FX2 needs firmware upload on every USB replug — no onboard flash
lesson: Always run fxload with -d (lowercase) flag before any JTAG operation. Use -D for device path.

action: Test UART echo via /dev/tty.usbserial-1120 at 115200 baud
symptom: No echo response from FPGA, minicom shows sent chars but nothing received
root_cause: UART headers on QMTech board are NOT soldered — physical connection absent
lesson: UART requires soldered pin headers on J-series connectors. No headers = no signal.

action: Identiy U2 CPLD pinout on Platform Cable USB II board
symptom: Need to understand signal routing between FX2, CPLD, and JTAG header
root_cause: U2 is XC2C256 CPLD that routes TDI/TDO/TMS/TCK between FX2 USB chip and JTAG header
lesson: Platform Cable USB II has 3 chips: FX2 (USB), CPLD (routing), and JTAG header. CPLD is the bridge.

symptom: openFPGALoader returns error: cable 'xpc' not supported
root_cause: openFPGALoader does not support Xilinx Platform Cable USB II (xpc) — only FTDI-based cables
lesson: NEVER use openFPGALoader with --cable xpc. Use native jtag_program/jtag_switcher instead.

action: Order replacement equipment: new Platform Cable USB II + soldering kit for UART headers
symptom: Current cable has dead CPLD, UART headers not soldered
root_cause: Hardware limitations cannot be fixed with software
lesson: When hardware is defective, order replacement — don't waste time on software workarounds

action: Compare original xpc.c with refactored version — same CPLD version bug
symptom: Both original and refactored jtag_program read CPLD version 0xFFFE
root_cause: Bug is in hardware (CPLD), not in software — both code paths use identical USB control transfers
lesson: If original and refactored code produce same bug, problem is below the software layer

root_cause: 0xFFFE means CPLD GPIF bus is stuck high (all 1s except LSB) — CPLD is not responding to version query, TDO routing through CPLD is dead
lesson: CPLD version 0xFFFE = dead CPLD. TDI works (writing succeeds) but TDO is dead (reading fails). Don't debug software for this.

action: Add debug command to jtag_switcher for config read path diagnosis
symptom: jtag_program reads IDCODE 0x13631093 (TDO works for IR), but config register reads via CFG_IN/CFG_OUT return all zeros
root_cause: PENDING — debug command tests: IR vs CFG IDCODE, raw TDO bytes, variable NOPs, 64-bit read offset
lesson: CPLD 0xFFFE may be degraded but not dead — short TDO scans (IR IDCODE 32-bit) work, long scans (CFG_IN pipeline) may fail. Need differential diagnosis.

action: Full debug diagnosis on new DLC10 cable — all 6 tests passed
symptom: Config register reads were assumed broken due to CPLD 0xFFFE
root_cause: CPLD 0xFFFE is NORMAL for DLC10 clones — it does NOT mean dead TDO. All config register reads work perfectly.
lesson: NEVER assume CPLD 0xFFFE = dead cable. Always test with actual IDCODE + config register reads. The CPLD version register is unreliable on clones.

action: Read STAT register — FPGA configured, DONE=1, no errors
symptom: Needed to verify FPGA configuration state
root_cause: FPGA is configured with some bitstream. DONE=1, MMCM locked, DCI matched, no CRC errors, part NOT secured (readback allowed).
lesson: STAT register 0x401079FC = healthy configured FPGA. Readback should work since Part Secured = 0.

action: fxload works WITHOUT sudo on macOS for DLC10
symptom: Expected sudo to be required for USB access on macOS
root_cause: This specific macOS setup allows libusb access without root. Both fxload and jtag_switcher work without sudo.
lesson: On this machine, sudo is NOT required for JTAG tools. Try without sudo first, only add sudo if permission denied.

action: Full write cycle on DLC10 — 3.6 MB bitstream programmed successfully
symptom: Needed to verify write path works end-to-end
root_cause: jtag_switcher write command sent full bitstream, JSTART triggered, final IDCODE 0x13631093 matches.
lesson: Write path is 100% functional on DLC10. JPROGRAM -> CFG_IN -> JSTART sequence works.

action: Full readback cycle — 3754 frames x 101 words = 379154 words (1.4 MB) read successfully
symptom: Readback completes 100% (1516616 bytes read) but verify shows 333 mismatches at offset 0x0
root_cause: Readback WORKS (data read from FPGA), but bit alignment or byte ordering causes mismatches. NOT dead TDO!
lesson: Readback path is functional. Mismatches suggest frame offset or bit-reversal issue in comparison. Write works, read works, verify needs fix.
